Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Pretext: TypeScript library for multiline text measurement and layout 349 upvotes, 62 comments
  2. 15 Years of Forking 253 upvotes, 53 comments
  3. The curious case of retro demo scene graphics 286 upvotes, 69 comments
  4. Douglas Lenat's Automated Mathematician Source Code 48 upvotes, 7 comments
  5. Copilot edited an ad into my PR 1097 upvotes, 314 comments
  6. Hardware Image Compression 49 upvotes, 9 comments
  7. Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman Equation: Reinforcement Learning and Diffusion Models 100 upvotes, 27 comments
  8. Voyager 1 runs on 69 KB of memory and an 8-track tape recorder 618 upvotes, 228 comments
  9. Mathematical methods and human thought in the age of AI 102 upvotes, 35 comments
  10. How to Turn Anything into a Router 165 upvotes, 62 comments
  11. Ninja is a small build system with a focus on speed 57 upvotes, 17 comments
  12. VHDL's Crown Jewel 103 upvotes, 37 comments
  13. Comprehensive C++ Hashmap Benchmarks (2022) 34 upvotes, 9 comments
  14. I use excalidraw to manage my diagrams for my blog 180 upvotes, 81 comments
  15. Philly courts will ban all smart eyeglasses starting next week 348 upvotes, 170 comments
  16. "Over 1.5 million GitHub PRs have had ads injected into them by Copilot" 38 upvotes, 12 comments
  17. Build123d: A Python CAD programming library 6 upvotes, 0 comments
  18. Parrots pack twice as many neurons as primate brains of the same mass 107 upvotes, 52 comments
  19. ChatGPT won't let you type until Cloudflare reads your React state 835 upvotes, 543 comments
  20. Ghostmoon.app – The Swiss Army Knife for your macOS menu bar 117 upvotes, 89 comments
  21. Spring Boot Done Right: Lessons from a 400-Module Codebase 46 upvotes, 31 comments
  22. In Math, Rigor Is Vital. But Are Digitized Proofs Taking It Too Far? 27 upvotes, 16 comments
  23. Midnight train from GA: A view of America from the tracks as airports struggle 135 upvotes, 120 comments
  24. Coding agents could make free software matter again 242 upvotes, 246 comments
  25. C++26 is done: ISO C++ standards meeting Trip Report 281 upvotes, 296 comments
  26. My MacBook keyboard is broken and it's insanely expensive to fix 301 upvotes, 350 comments
  27. How the AI Bubble Bursts 276 upvotes, 329 comments
  28. The First Video Game Was Just a Box in the Corner of a Bar 28 upvotes, 25 comments
  29. How Reverse Game Theory Could Solve the Housing Shortage 13 upvotes, 13 comments
  30. The coming PLG to SLG apocalypse 6 upvotes, 5 comments
